It is a very useful tool for checking identifiability before parameter estimation. I went through all the examples and tested it on a simple model and the package worked pretty well. However, I noticed that I cannot include any algebra equations when I define the model, otherwise errors occur. Only differential equations and output equations are allowed to be included. For many models, the differential equations become onerous after substituting all variables based on algebra equations. Furthermore, some variables are not easily substituted by solving all algebra equations. Is it possible to include algebra equations when defining the model?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/376/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/376/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/2566023875","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/376#issuecomment-2566023875","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/376","id":2566023875,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEU_GA86Y8mrD","user":{"login":"pogudingleb","id":24942839,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI0OTQyODM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/24942839?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb","html_url":"https://github.com/pogudingleb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-31T00:08:52Z","updated_at":"2024-12-31T00:08:52Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"Thank you for using the software and providing feedback!

For the moment, algebraic constraints are not allowed (in full generality this is a complicated even in theory). If you would like to avoid substituting by hand, you can use the interface via `ModelingToolkit` and use `SymbolicUtils` for performing a substitution. Here is an example of the code

```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability
using ModelingToolkit, SymbolicUtils

@parameters a
@variables t, x1(t), x2(t), z(t)
D = Differential(t)

eqs = [D(x1) ~ z^2, D(x2) ~ a * z + x1]

subst = Dict(z => x2 + a)

eqs_transformed = [substitute(e, subst) for e in eqs]

ode = ODESystem(eqs_transformed, t, name = :dae)

println(assess_identifiability(ode, measured_quantities = [x1]))
```

If the substitution is not possible, then typically one should change the coordinates somehow to make it possible but this depends on the system at hand. If you have a particular system in mind, feel free to post it here or send to me (`gleb.pogudin@polytechnique.edu`).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/2566023875/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

using ModelingToolkit
using StructuralIdentifiability

    @variables X(t)
    @parameters p d
    eqs = [
        D(X) ~ p - d*X
    ]
    @mtkbuild osys = ODESystem(eqs, t)
    measured_quantities = [X]    
    funcs_to_check = [osys.p]

    SI.assess_identifiability(osys; measured_quantities, funcs_to_check)
end
```

using ModelingToolkit
using StructuralIdentifiability
const SI = StructuralIdentifiability

function testSI()
    t = ModelingToolkit.t_nounits
    D = ModelingToolkit.D_nounits
    @variables X(t)
    @parameters p d
    eqs = [
        D(X) ~ p - d*X
    ]
    @mtkbuild osys = ODESystem(eqs, t)
    measured_quantities = [X]    
    funcs_to_check = [osys.p]

    SI.assess_identifiability(osys; measured_quantities, funcs_to_check)
end
```

This resulted in the following code:

```
using StructuralIdentifiability
using OrderedCollections

# Define the ODE model with variables and parameters 
ode = @ODEmodel begin
    # Define the state derivatives
    a'(t) = p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t),
    b'(t) = p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t),
    nb'(t) = -nb(t) * n_ba * ((p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t)) / b(t)) * (1 - nb(t)),
    pa1'(t) = pa1(t) * n_a * ((p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t)) / a(t)) * (1 - pa1(t)),
    pa2'(t) = pa2(t) * n_ab * ((p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t)) / a(t)) * (1 - pa2(t)),
    pb'(t) = pb(t) * n_b * ((p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t)) / b(t)) * (1 - pb(t)),
    
    # Define the outputs
    y1(t) = a(t),  # First measurable output
    y2(t) = b(t)   # Second measurable output
end

# Specify the known initial conditions with variables 
known_initial_conditions = [a, b]

global_identifiability_results = assess_identifiability(
    ode,
    known_ic = known_initial_conditions
)

# Display the identifiability results
println("\nGlobal Identifiability Assessment Results:")
println("----------------------------------------")
for (key, value) in global_identifiability_results
    println("$key => $value")
end

end

requested"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-08T11:21:43Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T12:21:08Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I'm trying to assess the identifiability of reaction rates in a typical enzyme reaction model with hydrolysis. The measured quantity is an accumulated weight of several species over time. Unfortunately, `assess_identifiability` gets stuck while computing IO-equations for this model. Is this a local issue, or is there something intrinsically difficult about solving this problem? This is my code: 
```julia

using Catalyst
using StructuralIdentifiability

#creating a model
@parameters t #time
vars = @variables E(t) S(t) ES(t) P(t) #species
@parameters k_a k_d k_c


#underlying model describing the data
# binding and unbinding of enzyme and substrate:
model = @reaction_network begin
    @parameters wE wS wP
    k_a, E + S --> ES
    k_d, ES --> S + E
    k_c, ES --> P + E
end



#known initial conditions
known_ic = [E, S, ES, P]


#the measured quantities are the sum of the weights of the species
@unpack wE, wS, wP = model
measured_quantities = [(wE+wS)*ES + wP*P + wS*S]

#convert the model to an ODE system
ODE = convert(ODESystem, model)
StructuralIdentifiability.assess_identifiability(ODE, measured_quantities = measured_quantities; funcs_to_check = [k_a, k_d, k_c])
```
Thanks in advance!

Thanks for the interesting example! Indeed, the model is small but due to its nonlinearity direct identifiability computation gets stuck. A possible workaround is to use a conservation law `E + ES = C` in order to eliminate `E`. The resulting model is equivalent to the original one but much easier for the computation. Here is the code
```julia
ode = @ODEmodel(
       S'(t) = k_d * ES(t) - k_a * S(t) * (C - S(t)),
       ES'(t) = -k_c * ES(t) - k_d * ES(t) + k_a * (C - S(t)) * S(t),
       P'(t) = k_c * ES(t),
       y(t) = (wE + wS) * ES(t) + wP * P(t) + wS * S(t)
)
assess_identifiability(ode, known_ic = [S, ES, P, C - S])
```
We want to have this conversion to be done automatically but not yet there.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/2399693091/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

This resulted in the following code:

```
using StructuralIdentifiability
using OrderedCollections

# Define the ODE model with variables and parameters 
ode = @ODEmodel begin
    # Define the state derivatives
    a'(t) = p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t),
    b'(t) = p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t),
    nb'(t) = -nb(t) * n_ba * ((p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t)) / b(t)) * (1 - nb(t)),
    pa1'(t) = pa1(t) * n_a * ((p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t)) / a(t)) * (1 - pa1(t)),
    pa2'(t) = pa2(t) * n_ab * ((p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t)) / a(t)) * (1 - pa2(t)),
    pb'(t) = pb(t) * n_b * ((p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t)) / b(t)) * (1 - pb(t)),
    
    # Define the outputs
    y1(t) = a(t),  # First measurable output
    y2(t) = b(t)   # Second measurable output
end

# Specify the known initial conditions with variables 
known_initial_conditions = [a, b]

global_identifiability_results = assess_identifiability(
    ode,
    known_ic = known_initial_conditions
)

# Display the identifiability results
println("\nGlobal Identifiability Assessment Results:")
println("----------------------------------------")
for (key, value) in global_identifiability_results
    println("$key => $value")
end
```

Thanks for reporting! I think that the reason for this behaviour is that `@ODEmodel` macro has slightly different syntax, it expects parenthesis afterwards, not `begin ... end`. So if you replace
```Julia
ode = @ODEmodel begin
    # Define the state derivatives
    a'(t) = p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t),
    b'(t) = p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t),
    nb'(t) = -nb(t) * n_ba * ((p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t)) / b(t)) * (1 - nb(t)),
    pa1'(t) = pa1(t) * n_a * ((p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t)) / a(t)) * (1 - pa1(t)),
    pa2'(t) = pa2(t) * n_ab * ((p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t)) / a(t)) * (1 - pa2(t)),
    pb'(t) = pb(t) * n_b * ((p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t)) / b(t)) * (1 - pb(t)),
    
    # Define the outputs
    y1(t) = a(t),  # First measurable output
    y2(t) = b(t)   # Second measurable output
end
```

with 

```Julia
ode = @ODEmodel(
    # Define the state derivatives
    a'(t) = p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t),
    b'(t) = p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t),
    nb'(t) = -nb(t) * n_ba * ((p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t)) / b(t)) * (1 - nb(t)),
    pa1'(t) = pa1(t) * n_a * ((p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t)) / a(t)) * (1 - pa1(t)),
    pa2'(t) = pa2(t) * n_ab * ((p_a * s_0 * nb(t) - d_a * pa1(t)) / a(t)) * (1 - pa2(t)),
    pb'(t) = pb(t) * n_b * ((p_b * pa2(t) - d_b * pb(t)) / b(t)) * (1 - pb(t)),
    
    # Define the outputs
    y1(t) = a(t),  # First measurable output
    y2(t) = b(t)   # Second measurable output
)
```
everything will work.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/2380199490/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-07-20T20:23:15Z","updated_at":"2024-08-19T22:52:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug 🐞**

Following the example on [this page](https://docs.sciml.ai/ModelingToolkit/stable/tutorials/parameter_identifiability/) results in

    ERROR: Measured quantities (output functions) were not provided and no outputs were found.

I'm not sure that this package is really to blame.  Maybe @pogudingleb could provide some insight?

**Expected behavior**

I would have thought that marking `y1(t)` and `y2(t)` with `[output = true]` would be enough to communicate that these are outputs.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

I've just copied the example code, but run it in a temp environment:

```julia
using Pkg
Pkg.activate(; temp=true)
Pkg.add(["StructuralIdentifiability", "ModelingToolkit"])


using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit
using ModelingToolkit: t_nounits as t, D_nounits as D

@mtkmodel Biohydrogenation begin
    @variables begin
        x4(t)
        x5(t)
        x6(t)
        x7(t)
        y1(t), [output = true]
        y2(t), [output = true]
    end
    @parameters begin
        k5
        k6
        k7
        k8
        k9
        k10
    end
    # define equations
    @equations begin
        D(x4) ~ -k5 * x4 / (k6 + x4)
        D(x5) ~ k5 * x4 / (k6 + x4) - k7 * x5 / (k8 + x5 + x6)
        D(x6) ~ k7 * x5 / (k8 + x5 + x6) - k9 * x6 * (k10 - x6) / k10
        D(x7) ~ k9 * x6 * (k10 - x6) / k10
        y1 ~ x4
        y2 ~ x5
    end
end

# define the system
@mtkbuild de = Biohydrogenation()

# query local identifiability
# we pass the ode-system
local_id_all = assess_local_identifiability(de, prob_threshold = 0.99)
```

working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-07-20T20:23:15Z","updated_at":"2024-07-20T21:51:27Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug 🐞**

Following the example on [this page](https://docs.sciml.ai/ModelingToolkit/stable/tutorials/parameter_identifiability/) results in

    ERROR: Measured quantities (output functions) were not provided and no outputs were found.

I'm not sure that this package is really to blame.  Maybe @pogudingleb could provide some insight?

**Expected behavior**

I would have thought that marking `y1(t)` and `y2(t)` with `[output = true]` would be enough to communicate that these are outputs.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

I've just copied the example code, but run it in a temp environment:

```julia
using Pkg
Pkg.activate(; temp=true)
Pkg.add(["StructuralIdentifiability", "ModelingToolkit"])


using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit
using ModelingToolkit: t_nounits as t, D_nounits as D

@mtkmodel Biohydrogenation begin
    @variables begin
        x4(t)
        x5(t)
        x6(t)
        x7(t)
        y1(t), [output = true]
        y2(t), [output = true]
    end
    @parameters begin
        k5
        k6
        k7
        k8
        k9
        k10
    end
    # define equations
    @equations begin
        D(x4) ~ -k5 * x4 / (k6 + x4)
        D(x5) ~ k5 * x4 / (k6 + x4) - k7 * x5 / (k8 + x5 + x6)
        D(x6) ~ k7 * x5 / (k8 + x5 + x6) - k9 * x6 * (k10 - x6) / k10
        D(x7) ~ k9 * x6 * (k10 - x6) / k10
        y1 ~ x4
        y2 ~ x5
    end
end

# define the system
@mtkbuild de = Biohydrogenation()

# query local identifiability
# we pass the ode-system
local_id_all = assess_local_identifiability(de, prob_threshold = 0.99)
```

Thanks for reporting! This is indeed a big in the interface, this particular way of defining models does not quite work in the current versions. I am refactoring right now ModelingToolkit interface, I will fix this as well.

For the time being, you can just pass the observed quantities to the function directly like this

```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit
using ModelingToolkit: t_nounits as t, D_nounits as D

@mtkmodel Biohydrogenation begin
    @variables begin
        x4(t)
        x5(t)
        x6(t)
        x7(t)
        y1(t), [output = true]
        y2(t), [output = true]
    end
    @parameters begin
        k5
        k6
        k7
        k8
        k9
        k10
    end
    # define equations
    @equations begin
        D(x4) ~ -k5 * x4 / (k6 + x4)
        D(x5) ~ k5 * x4 / (k6 + x4) - k7 * x5 / (k8 + x5 + x6)
        D(x6) ~ k7 * x5 / (k8 + x5 + x6) - k9 * x6 * (k10 - x6) / k10
        D(x7) ~ k9 * x6 * (k10 - x6) / k10
    end
end

# define the system
@mtkbuild de = Biohydrogenation()

x4, x5, x6, x7 = unknowns(de)

# query local identifiability
# we pass the ode-system
local_id_all = assess_local_identifiability(de, measured_quantities = [x4, x5], prob_threshold = 0.99)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ModelingToolkit.jl/issues/comments/2241304953/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

the bug 🐞**

Following the example on [this page](https://docs.sciml.ai/ModelingToolkit/stable/tutorials/parameter_identifiability/) results in

    ERROR: Measured quantities (output functions) were not provided and no outputs were found.

I'm not sure that this package is really to blame.  Maybe @pogudingleb could provide some insight?

**Expected behavior**

I would have thought that marking `y1(t)` and `y2(t)` with `[output = true]` would be enough to communicate that these are outputs.

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

I've just copied the example code, but run it in a temp environment:

```julia
using Pkg
Pkg.activate(; temp=true)
Pkg.add(["StructuralIdentifiability", "ModelingToolkit"])


using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit
using ModelingToolkit: t_nounits as t, D_nounits as D

@mtkmodel Biohydrogenation begin
    @variables begin
        x4(t)
        x5(t)
        x6(t)
        x7(t)
        y1(t), [output = true]
        y2(t), [output = true]
    end
    @parameters begin
        k5
        k6
        k7
        k8
        k9
        k10
    end
    # define equations
    @equations begin
        D(x4) ~ -k5 * x4 / (k6 + x4)
        D(x5) ~ k5 * x4 / (k6 + x4) - k7 * x5 / (k8 + x5 + x6)
        D(x6) ~ k7 * x5 / (k8 + x5 + x6) - k9 * x6 * (k10 - x6) / k10
        D(x7) ~ k9 * x6 * (k10 - x6) / k10
        y1 ~ x4
        y2 ~ x5
    end
end

# define the system
@mtkbuild de = Biohydrogenation()

# query local identifiability
# we pass the ode-system
local_id_all = assess_local_identifiability(de, prob_threshold = 0.99)
```

Since posting this, I took a look too and found `ModelingToolkit.observed`. Is this what you need? In particular, could you change the signatures in your extension to use that as a default value, like this:
> 
> ```julia
> function StructuralIdentifiability.find_identifiable_functions(
>     ode::ModelingToolkit.ODESystem;
>     measured_quantities = ModelingToolkit.observed(ode),
> ```
> 
> Thanks for your help!

Thanks! We indeed used `observed` before (but in a less nice way as you proposed) but this code was lost in some transformations and, apparently, was not covered in the tests. I will fix this for the next release.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ModelingToolkit.jl/issues/comments/2241312346/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-06-02T21:25:32Z","updated_at":"2024-06-02T21:27:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"To designate that some initial conditions are known. E.g. if I do
```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit

@parameters r1, r2, c1, c2, beta1, beta2, chi1, chi2
@variables t, x1(t), x2(t), y(t), u(t)

D = Differential(t)
eqs = [
    D(x1) ~ r1 * x1 * (1 - c1 * x1) + beta1 * x1 * x2 / (chi1 + x2) + u,
    D(x2) ~ r2 * x2 * (1 - c2 * x2) + beta2 * x1 * x2 / (chi2 + x1),
]

measured_quantities = [y ~ x1]

ode_mtk = ODESystem(eqs, t, name = :mutualist)

assess_identifiability(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities) # works

assess_identifiability(ode_mtk; measured_quantities = measured_quantities, known_ic = [x1])
```
I get a 
```
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching assess_identifiability(::ODESystem; measured_quantities::Vector{Equation}, known_ic::Vector{Num})
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/2144026521","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324#issuecomment-2144026521","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324","id":2144026521,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEU_GA85_yz-Z","user":{"login":"pogudingleb","id":24942839,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI0OTQyODM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/24942839?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb","html_url":"https://github.com/pogudingleb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-02T21:27:29Z","updated_at":"2024-06-02T21:27:29Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"@TorkelE

using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit

@parameters r1, r2, c1, c2, beta1, beta2, chi1, chi2
@variables t, x1(t), x2(t), y(t), u(t)

D = Differential(t)
eqs = [
    D(x1) ~ r1 * x1 * (1 - c1 * x1) + beta1 * x1 * x2 / (chi1 + x2) + u,
    D(x2) ~ r2 * x2 * (1 - c2 * x2) + beta2 * x1 * x2 / (chi2 + x1),
]

measured_quantities = [y ~ x1]

ode_mtk = ODESystem(eqs, t, name = :mutualist)

assess_identifiability(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities) # works

assess_identifiability(ode_mtk; measured_quantities = measured_quantities, known_ic = [x1])
```
I get a 
```
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching assess_identifiability(::ODESystem; measured_quantities::Vector{Equation}, known_ic::Vector{Num})
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/2156808388","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324#issuecomment-2156808388","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324","id":2156808388,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEU_GA86AjkjE","user":{"login":"pogudingleb","id":24942839,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI0OTQyODM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/24942839?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb","html_url":"https://github.com/pogudingleb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-09T22:01:51Z","updated_at":"2024-06-09T22:01:51Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"@TorkelE 
I have a PR addressing this issue, here is the current interface. For a model defined as
```Julia
using StructuralIdentifiability
using ModelingToolkit
using Logging

@parameters r1, r2, c1, c2, beta1, beta2, chi1, chi2
@variables t, x1(t), x2(t), y(t), u(t)

D = Differential(t)

eqs = [
    D(x1) ~ r1 * x1 * (1 - c1 * x1) + beta1 * x1 * x2 / (chi1 + x2) + u,
    D(x2) ~ r2 * x2 * (1 - c2 * x2) + beta2 * x1 * x2 / (chi2 + x1),
]

measured_quantities = [y ~ x1]

ode_mtk = ODESystem(eqs, t, name = :mutualist)
```
One can provide `known_ic` argument to `assess_identifiability` and `find_identifiable_functions`.

```julia
assess_identifiability(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities, known_ic = [x2])
```
will produce
```
OrderedCollections.OrderedDict{SymbolicUtils.BasicSymbolic{Real}, Symbol}(
    x1(0) => :globally, 
    x2(0) => :globally, 
    u(0) => :globally, 
    chi1 => :globally, 
    r1 => :globally, 
    beta1 => :globally, 
    c1 => :globally, 
    c2 => :globally, 
    chi2 => :globally, 
    r2 => :globally, 
    beta2 => :globally)
```
and
```julia
find_identifiable_functions(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities, known_ic = [x2])
```
will produce
```
Num[beta2, r2, chi2, c2, c1, beta1, r1, chi1, u(0), x2(0), x1(0)]
```
**Note** that, if known initial conditions are provided, then the returned identifiability results are valid at `t = 0`, this is why `t = 0` is substituted in the results above.

Let me know if this interface is convenient.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/2156808388/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

assess_identifiability(ode_mtk; measured_quantities = measured_quantities, known_ic = [x1])
```

```
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching assess_identifiability(::ODESystem; measured_quantities::Vector{Equation}, known_ic::Vector{Num})

working"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-06-02T21:25:32Z","updated_at":"2024-06-10T19:12:55Z","closed_at":"2024-06-10T19:12:55Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"To designate that some initial conditions are known. E.g. if I do
```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit

@parameters r1, r2, c1, c2, beta1, beta2, chi1, chi2
@variables t, x1(t), x2(t), y(t), u(t)

D = Differential(t)
eqs = [
    D(x1) ~ r1 * x1 * (1 - c1 * x1) + beta1 * x1 * x2 / (chi1 + x2) + u,
    D(x2) ~ r2 * x2 * (1 - c2 * x2) + beta2 * x1 * x2 / (chi2 + x1),
]

measured_quantities = [y ~ x1]

ode_mtk = ODESystem(eqs, t, name = :mutualist)

assess_identifiability(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities) # works

assess_identifiability(ode_mtk; measured_quantities = measured_quantities, known_ic = [x1])
```
I get a 
```
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching assess_identifiability(::ODESystem; measured_quantities::Vector{Equation}, known_ic::Vector{Num})
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/324/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}



I am new to both structural identifiability analysis and Julia. So I hope the answer to my question is not completely obvious. I am able to obtain the local SI results but when commuting the global ones, the output in the terminal always stalls at "Computing IO-equations". I have left it running on an HPC over night. 

Here's the code:

```julia
#import Pkg; 
#using Pkg
#Pkg.add("StructuralIdentifiability")
#Pkg.add("OrdinaryDiffEq")

using StructuralIdentifiability, OrdinaryDiffEq

odeMyModel = @ODEmodel(
	Complex'(t) = 1/C*(((2*kon*free_receptor(t)*Drug(t)-koff*Complex(t))*C) - (ke_Complex*Complex(t)*C) - ((kon_2*free_receptor(t)*Complex(t)-2*koff*Complex_2(t))*C)),
	Complex_2'(t) = 1/C*(((kon_2*free_receptor(t)*Complex(t)-2*koff*Complex_2(t))*C) - (ke_Complex_2*Complex_2(t)*C)),
        Drug'(t) = 1/C*(-(ke_Drug*Drug(t)*C) - ((2*kon*free_receptor(t)*Drug(t)-koff*Complex(t))*C) + (45/100*ka*Drug_SC(t))),
        free_receptor'(t) = 1/C*(-((2*kon*free_receptor(t)*Drug(t)-koff*Complex(t))*C) + (66/2500*C) - (kdeg_free_receptor*free_receptor(t)*C) - ((kon_2*free_receptor(t)*Complex(t)-2*koff*Complex_2(t))*C)),
	Drug_SC'(t) = -(45/100*ka*Drug_SC(t)) - ((1-45/100)*ka*Drug_SC(t)) + u_SC(t),
        y1(t) = Drug(t),                                   # output free Drug
        y2(t) = free_receptor(t) + Complex(t) + 2*Complex_2(t)      # output total receptor
)

local_id_all = assess_local_identifiability(odeMyModel, 0.99)

assess_identifiability(odeMyModel,0.99) 
```

The parameters, state variables, inputs and outputs seem to be correctly recognized:

```julia
[ Info: Summary of the model:
[ Info: State variables: Drug, free_receptor, Complex, Complex_2, Drug_SC
[ Info: Parameters: ke_Complex_2, kdeg_free_receptor, ka, kon, ke_Drug, ke_Complex, kon_2, koff, C
[ Info: Inputs: u_SC
[ Info: Outputs: y1, y2
[ Info: Assessing local identifiability
[ Info: Local identifiability assessed in 0.091845932 seconds
[ Info: Assessing global identifiability
[ Info: Computing IO-equations
```
But it doesn't move past there.

Also, I was looking to include initial conditions but have failed in the attempt in multiple ways. I think it might not be possible yet. If that is the case, can you recommend an alternative for SI - maybe even additionally allowing bounds for parameters?

Thanks a lot in advance for your help! v2.0.0 - Update to Node20

What's Changed

  • update to node20 by @​ranocha in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Note the rationale for the breaking change was discussed in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Dependencies and misc

  • Bump nock from 13.5.1 to 13.5.4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#224
  • Bump @​types/semver from 7.5.6 to 7.5.8 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#222
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.11.16 to 20.11.30 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#226
  • Bump the version number to 2.0.0 in package.json and package-lock.json by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#228

New Contributors

  • @​ranocha made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#209

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.6...v2.0.0

v1.9.6: Fix Apple Silicon installation

What's Changed

  • Fix the Apple Silicon (macOS aarch64 / arm64) URLs for Julia nightly by @​benlorenz in julia-actions/setup-julia#220
  • put quotes on readme version examples by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#203

Deps & CI

  • Bump actions/cache from 3 to 4 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#215
  • Bump @​types/node from 20.10.6 to 20.11.16 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#213
  • Bump ts-jest from 29.1.1 to 29.1.2 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#212
  • Bump nock from 13.4.0 to 13.5.1 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#211
  • Bump semver from 7.5.4 to 7.6.0 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#217
  • CI: Increase the Dependabot "number of open PRs" limit by @​DilumAluthge in julia-actions/setup-julia#218
  • Bump prettier from 3.1.1 to 3.2.5 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#216
  • Bump @​types/jest from 29.5.11 to 29.5.12 by @​dependabot in julia-actions/setup-julia#210
  • 1.9.6 and compat bump for node semver package by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#221

New Contributors

  • @​benlorenz made their first contribution in julia-actions/setup-julia#220

Full Changelog: https://github.com/julia-actions/setup-julia/compare/v1.9.5...v1.9.6

v1.9.5: Maintain mtime of julia build to avoid re-precompilation

A big part of fixing JuliaLang/julia#50667 for julia <1.11 as maintaining the mtime of the julia source files means cached /compiled caches won't be invalidated in new runs where julia has been reinstalled.

What's Changed

  • add test for julia install and mtime maintenance by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#200
  • extract julia directly to tool path to maintain mtimes by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#196
  • v1.9.5 by @​IanButterworth in julia-actions/setup-julia#201

The SciML tutorial for StructuralIdentifiability seems to indicate that we can mark variables as inputs or outputs, so that they should behave as knowns when identifiying unknown parameters, as in https://docs.sciml.ai/ModelingToolkit/dev/tutorials/parameter_identifiability/ . 

**Minimal Reproducible Example 👇**

Taken directly from the mentioned tutorial:
```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit
@variables t
D = Differential(t)

@mtkmodel GoodwinOscillator begin
    @parameters begin
        b
        c
        α
        β
        γ
        δ
        σ
    end
    @variables begin
        x1(t)
        x2(t)
        x3(t)
        x4(t)
        y(t), [output = true]
        y2(t), [output = true]
        u1(t), [input = true]
        u2(t), [input = true]
    end
    @equations begin
        D(x1) ~ -b * x1 + 1 / (c + x4)
        D(x2) ~ α * x1 - β * x2 - u1
        D(x3) ~ γ * x2 - δ * x3 + u2
        D(x4) ~ σ * x4 * (γ * x2 - δ * x3) / x3
        y ~ x1 + x2
        y2 ~ x2
    end
end

@named ode = GoodwinOscillator()
ode = complete(ode)

# check only 2 parameters
to_check = [ode.b, ode.c]

global_id = assess_identifiability(ode, funcs_to_check = to_check, p = 0.9)
```

**Error & Stacktrace ⚠️**

```julia
[ Info: Measured quantities are not provided, trying to find the outputs in input ODE.
```
[followed by other info statements about calculation progress]

```julia
OrderedCollections.OrderedDict{Num, Symbol} with 2 entries:
  b => :globally
  c => :globally
```
So, the result from the tutorial is as described, but relies on this automatic detection of the ODE outputs. In the somewhat-more-complicated example I am interested in, **SI seems unable to infer from my ODE specification what the outputs are**, although I have tried to closely follow the tutorial syntax, including marking variables with `[input = true]` or `[output = true]`.

A closely related but possibly separate question:
Given the above, I would like to better understand the syntax requirements for marking variables as "measured". In my model of interest, I have several knowns or parameters calibrated by different techniques; should I somehow mark these as known?
In the tutorials and docs I have found, the keyword argument is constructed with
```julia
measured = [de.y ~ de.x1 + de.x2, de.y2 ~ de.x2]
assess_identifiability(de, measured_quantities = measured)
```
It feels clunky to me to introduce new auxiliary variables with `y ~ x1+x1` and `y2~x2` to mark as measured, instead of just listing `x1+x2` and `x2` as measured, and I am unclear how this should work for e.g. known, controllable system inputs, of which I have several. **Do I just need to introduce a bunch of new auxiliary variables so I can mark them all as measured?**

Some references on the subject are:

I have implemented the minimal glucose model (Bergman et al 1979) with Catalyst.jl, from which I get an ODESystem object. As described in my paper of reference, the small model includes interpolated insulin data (to avoid regulation modeling):

```
using Catalyst, DifferentialEquations, StructuralIdentifiability, Interpolations

tticks = [0.0, 15.0, 30.0, 60.0, 90.0, 120.0, 180.0, 240.0, 300.0]
datamean = [6.12,18.10, 32.57, 25.77, 27.94, 33.47, 35.18, 39.88, 46.20] 

# minimal model
Iintpol(t) = linear_interpolation(tticks,datamean)(t)
@register_symbolic Iintpol(t)
minimalModel_rn = @reaction_network minimalmodel begin
    B_0,     ∅   ⇒   G_p              # hepatic glucose production
    X,       G_p --> ∅                   # glucose insulin-dependent regulation
    k_greg,  G_p --> 2*G_p      # glucose "self-regulation"
    k_ipan*Iintpol(t), ∅ ⇒ X    # blood insulin --> tissue insulin            
    k_ireg, X --> 2*X                  # insulin "self-regulation"
end B_0 k_ipan k_ireg k_greg

minimalModel_ode = convert(ODESytem, minimalModel_rn)
```
When I try to perform a full structural analysis, I obtain an issue because of Iintpol(t):
```
@variables t y(t)
assess_identifiability(minimalModel_ode,measured_quantities = [y ~ states(minimalModel_ode)[1]]) # G_p is the observable
```
output: 
```
[ Info: Preproccessing `ModelingToolkit.ODESystem` object
ArgumentError: Function Iintpol is not supported
```

Is there any workaround for this case?

is an attempt to fix the modelling toolkit extension, and reintroduce support for the `mtk_to_si` function, which was removed in the last update.

Changes:
- `mtk_to_si` is no longer exported from the extension. But instead created in the main package (without methods) and methods then added in the extension.
- I have removed the `export` statements in the extension as these should not be there.
- I have renamed the extension to `ModelingToolkitSIExt`. Motivation: extensions should (ideally) have names unique to both packages involved. If two packages both created extensions called `ModelingToolkitExt` (no unthinkable), these will overwrite each other, causing errors if loaded simultaneously.

Unfortunately this does not work fully. This code:
```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit

@parameters r1, r2, c1, c2, beta1, beta2, chi1, chi2
@variables t, x1(t), x2(t), y(t), u(t)

D= Differential(t)

eqs = [
    D(x1) ~ r1 * x1 * (1 - c1 * x1) + beta1 * x1 * x2 / (chi1 + x2) + u,
    D(x2) ~ r2 * x2 * (1 - c2 * x2) + beta2 * x1 * x2 / (chi2 + x1),
]

measured_quantities = [y ~ x1]

ode_mtk = ODESystem(eqs, t, name = :mutualist)

local_id_1 = assess_identifiability(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities)
global_id_1 = assess_local_identifiability(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities)


si_model = mtk_to_si(ode_mtk, measured_quantities)
global_id_2 = assess_identifiability(si_model)
```
generates a 
```
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching assess_identifiability(::Tuple{ODE{Nemo.QQMPolyRingElem}, Dict{SymbolicUtils.BasicSymbolic{…}, Nemo.QQMPolyRingElem}})

Closest candidates are:
  assess_identifiability(::ODESystem; measured_quantities, funcs_to_check, prob_threshold, loglevel)
   @ ModelingToolkitSIExt ~/.julia/dev/StructuralIdentifiability/ext/ModelingToolkitSIExt.jl:349
  assess_identifiability(::ODE{P}; funcs_to_check, prob_threshold, loglevel) where P<:AbstractAlgebra.MPolyRingElem{Nemo.QQFieldElem}
   @ StructuralIdentifiability ~/.julia/dev/StructuralIdentifiability/src/StructuralIdentifiability.jl:98

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ ~/Desktop/Julia Playground/Catalyst playground/Environment - Temporary/temproary_playground.jl:24
Some type information was truncated. Use `show(err)` to see complete types.
```
error.

This, however, works:
```julia
ode = @ODEmodel(
    x1'(t) = -(a01 + a21) * x1(t) + a12 * x2(t) + u(t),
    x2'(t) = a21 * x1(t) - a12 * x2(t) - x3(t) / b,
    x3'(t) = x3(t),
    y(t) = x2(t)
)
assess_identifiability(ode)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/280/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/280/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/1922205449","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/pull/280#issuecomment-1922205449","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/280","id":1922205449,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEU_GA85ykocJ","user":{"login":"pogudingleb","id":24942839,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI0OTQyODM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/24942839?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb","html_url":"https://github.com/pogudingleb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-01T20:38:48Z","updated_at":"2024-02-01T20:38:48Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"> This is an attempt to fix the modelling toolkit extension, and reintroduce support for the `mtk_to_si` function, which was removed in the last update.

is an attempt to fix the modelling toolkit extension, and reintroduce support for the `mtk_to_si` function, which was removed in the last update.

Changes:
- `mtk_to_si` is no longer exported from the extension. But instead created in the main package (without methods) and methods then added in the extension.
- I have removed the `export` statements in the extension as these should not be there.
- I have renamed the extension to `ModelingToolkitSIExt`. Motivation: extensions should (ideally) have names unique to both packages involved. If two packages both created extensions called `ModelingToolkitExt` (no unthinkable), these will overwrite each other, causing errors if loaded simultaneously.

Unfortunately this does not work fully. This code:
```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability, ModelingToolkit

@parameters r1, r2, c1, c2, beta1, beta2, chi1, chi2
@variables t, x1(t), x2(t), y(t), u(t)

D= Differential(t)

eqs = [
    D(x1) ~ r1 * x1 * (1 - c1 * x1) + beta1 * x1 * x2 / (chi1 + x2) + u,
    D(x2) ~ r2 * x2 * (1 - c2 * x2) + beta2 * x1 * x2 / (chi2 + x1),
]

measured_quantities = [y ~ x1]

ode_mtk = ODESystem(eqs, t, name = :mutualist)

local_id_1 = assess_identifiability(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities)
global_id_1 = assess_local_identifiability(ode_mtk, measured_quantities = measured_quantities)


si_model = mtk_to_si(ode_mtk, measured_quantities)
global_id_2 = assess_identifiability(si_model)
```
generates a 
```
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching assess_identifiability(::Tuple{ODE{Nemo.QQMPolyRingElem}, Dict{SymbolicUtils.BasicSymbolic{…}, Nemo.QQMPolyRingElem}})

Closest candidates are:
  assess_identifiability(::ODESystem; measured_quantities, funcs_to_check, prob_threshold, loglevel)
   @ ModelingToolkitSIExt ~/.julia/dev/StructuralIdentifiability/ext/ModelingToolkitSIExt.jl:349
  assess_identifiability(::ODE{P}; funcs_to_check, prob_threshold, loglevel) where P<:AbstractAlgebra.MPolyRingElem{Nemo.QQFieldElem}
   @ StructuralIdentifiability ~/.julia/dev/StructuralIdentifiability/src/StructuralIdentifiability.jl:98

Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ ~/Desktop/Julia Playground/Catalyst playground/Environment - Temporary/temproary_playground.jl:24
Some type information was truncated. Use `show(err)` to see complete types.
```
error.

This, however, works:
```julia
ode = @ODEmodel(
    x1'(t) = -(a01 + a21) * x1(t) + a12 * x2(t) + u(t),
    x2'(t) = a21 * x1(t) - a12 * x2(t) - x3(t) / b,
    x3'(t) = x3(t),
    y(t) = x2(t)
)
assess_identifiability(ode)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/280/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/280/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/1922366816","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/pull/280#issuecomment-1922366816","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/280","id":1922366816,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEU_GA85ylP1g","user":{"login":"pogudingleb","id":24942839,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI0OTQyODM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/24942839?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb","html_url":"https://github.com/pogudingleb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-01T22:20:36Z","updated_at":"2024-02-01T22:20:36Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"@TorkelE MWE:
```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability
mtk_to_si # ERROR: UndefVarError: `mtk_to_si` not defined
using ModelingToolkit
mtk_to_si # ERROR: UndefVarError: `mtk_to_si` not defined
```
I think this was caused by the latest update.

Currently, this is breaking tests in Catalyst, hence I discovered it: https://github.com/SciML/Catalyst.jl/actions/runs/7743939220/job/21116510775?pr=776

I checked the source code. I am not really sure, but when I make extensions I do not put export statements in the extension file. Instead, I create a `mtk_to_si` function (without methods) in the main file, and then add methods to that in the extension. E.g. in Catalyst's HomotopyContinuation extension I have this in Catalyst.jl
```julia
# HomotopyContinuation
function hc_steady_states end
export hc_steady_states
```
and this in homotopy_continuation_extension.jl
```julia
function Catalyst.hc_steady_states(rs::ReactionSystem, ps; filter_negative=true, neg_thres=-1e-20, u0=[], kwargs...)
    ss_poly = steady_state_polynomial(rs, ps, u0)
    sols = HC.real_solutions(HC.solve(ss_poly; kwargs...))
    reorder_sols!(sols, ss_poly, rs)
    return (filter_negative ? filter_negative_f(sols; neg_thres) : sols)
end
```
This might be worth a In this context, the functions assess identifiability or find all identifiable functions in parameters and initial conditions, respectively.

For example:
```julia
using StructuralIdentifiability: assess_identifiability_kic

ode = @ODEmodel(
    x1'(t) = a * x1(t) - b * x1(t) * x2(t),
    x2'(t) = -c * x2(t) + d * x1(t) * x2(t),
    y(t) = x1(t)
)

assess_identifiability_kic(ode, [x2])
```
will return
```
OrderedDict{Nemo.fmpq_mpoly, Symbol} with 6 entries:
  x1(0) => :globally
  x2(0) => :globally
  a     => :globally
  b     => :globally
  c     => :globally
  d     => :globally
```

Algorithmically, first identifiable functions are computed (`with_states=true`) and then the known initial conditions are added to them as generator of a fields following by an additional simplification step. The computation can be done more efficiently if the generators are added earlier, but this would complicate code quite a bit, not so clear if this is a good idea right now.

What is missing is the version of this functionality for local Release notes

Sourced from codecov/codecov-action's releases.

v4.0.0

v4 of the Codecov Action uses the CLI as the underlying upload. The CLI has helped to power new features including local upload, the global upload token, and new upcoming features.

Breaking Changes

  • The Codecov Action runs as a node20 action due to node16 deprecation. See this post from GitHub on how to migrate.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsupported. However, PRs made from forks to the upstream public repos will support tokenless (e.g. contributors to OS projects do not need the upstream repo's Codecov token). This doc shows instructions on how to add the Codecov token.
  • OS platforms have been added, though some may not be automatically detected. To see a list of platforms, see our CLI download page
  • Various arguments to the Action have been changed. Please be aware that the arguments match with the CLI's needs

v3 versions and below will not have access to CLI features (e.g. global upload token, ATS).

What's Changed

4.0.0-beta.2

Fixes

  • #1085 not adding -n if empty to do-upload command

4.0.0-beta.1

v4 represents a move from the universal uploader to the Codecov CLI. Although this will unlock new features for our users, the CLI is not yet at feature parity with the universal uploader.

Breaking Changes

  • No current support for aarch64 and alpine architectures.
  • Tokenless uploading is unsuported
  • Various arguments to the Action have been removed

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/checkout/compare/v3.5.1...v3.5.2

v3.5.1

What's Changed

  • Improve checkout performance on Windows runners by upgrading @​actions/github dependency by @​BrettDong in actions/checkout#1246

New Contributors

  • @​BrettDong made their first contribution in actions/checkout#1246

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from actions/checkout's changelog.

Changelog

v4.1.0

  • Add support for partial checkout filters

v4.0.0

  • Support fetching without the --progress option
  • Update to node20

v3.6.0

  • Fix: Mark test scripts with Bash'isms to be run via Bash
  • Add option to fetch tags even if fetch-depth > 0

v3.5.3

  • Fix: Checkout fail in self-hosted runners when faulty submodule are checked-in
  • Fix typos found by codespell
  • Add support for sparse checkouts

v3.5.2

  • Fix api endpoint for GHES

v3.5.1

  • Fix slow checkout on Windows

v3.5.0

  • Add new public key for known_hosts

v3.4.0

  • Upgrade codeql actions to v2
  • Upgrade dependencies
  • Upgrade @​actions/io

v3.3.0

  • Implement branch list using callbacks from exec function
  • Add in explicit reference to private checkout options
  • [Fix comment typos (that got added in #770)](actions/checkout#1057)

v3.2.0

  • Add GitHub Action to perform release
  • Fix status badge
  • Replace datadog/squid with ubuntu/squid Docker image
  • Wrap pipeline commands for submoduleForeach in quotes
  • Update @​actions/io to 1.1.2
  • Upgrading version to 3.2.0

v3.1.0

  • Use @​actions/core saveState and getState
  • Add github-server-url input

v3.0.2

If a parameter is known, it is easy to incorporate this through
```julia
si_ode = @ODEmodel(
    X1'(t) = p - k1*X1(t),
    X2'(t) = X1(t) -  X2(t),
    y1(t) = X2(t),
    y2(t) = p
)
```
However, having certain initial conditions being known is very plausible (especially in cases where you set up the experiments yourself, such as in chemistry). 

If https://github.com/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/236 gets solved (but I have no idea how hard that would be though), this would automatically become possible through:
```julia
si_ode = @ODEmodel(
    X1'(t) = p - k1*X1(t),
    X2'(t) = X1(t) -  X2(t),
    y1(t) = X2(t),
    y2(t) = X1(0)
)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/237/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/237/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/comments/1929352847","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/237#issuecomment-1929352847","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/237","id":1929352847,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEU_GA85y_5aP","user":{"login":"pogudingleb","id":24942839,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI0OTQyODM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/24942839?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb","html_url":"https://github.com/pogudingleb","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pogudingleb/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-06T11:48:22Z","updated_at":"2024-02-06T11:48:22Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"The functionality to add the known initial conditions (assuming that they are generic) has been added in release `0.5.4` (right now, for `@ODEmodel` macro only). {"action":"closed","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/236","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/236/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/236/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/236/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/236","id":1983738076,"node_id":"I_kwDOEU_GA852PXDc","number":236,"title":"Including initial conditions in equations","user":{"login":"TorkelE","id":18099310,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE4MDk5MzEw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/18099310?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE","html_url":"https://github.com/TorkelE","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/TorkelE/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":16,"created_at":"2023-11-08T14:27:28Z","updated_at":"2024-02-06T11:49:39Z","closed_at":"2024-02-06T11:49:39Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Is it possible to include initial conditions in the equations (in some sense, these are essentially parameters, right?). E.g something like:
```julia
si_ode = @ODEmodel(
    X1'(t) = p - k1*X1(t) + X1(0),
    X2'(t) = X1(t) -  X2(t),
    y1(t) = X2(t)
)
```
The reason I specifically am wondering about this is that, when reaction system have conservation laws (such as `X1 + X2 = C`), this can be used to eliminate a variable, e.g. X2. However, this introduces a new parameter `C`, which can be expressed `X1(0) + X2(0) = C`. Also, if `X2` (which is eliminated) happens to be a measured quantity, this would need to be replaced with something like `X1(0) + X2(0) - X1(t)`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/236/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/StructuralIdentifiability.jl/issues/236/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

addition to knowing the ODE system and time series data for some outputs, one frequently also knows the initial conditions for some of the states. It would be great to use this information to clarify the identifiability analysis.
For an algorithm based on input-output equations, an interesting discussion of this problem can be found in [this paper](https://doi.org/10.1016/S0005-1098(02)00302-3). To the best of my understanding, the paper does not give a complete algorithm but some ideas can be of interest.

Some interesting specific things we could do:
* fixed initial conditions can be used when verifying the rank of the Wronskian. If it is still of full rank, then identifiability with these fixed initial conditions is at least as good as without them (that is, we do not loose identifiability because of hitting a singular point).
* As suggested in the paper mentioned above, if the system is controllable from the fixed initial conditions, then again the result is at least as good.
* What would be interesting to see is if we could get a different result of elimination by making some initial conditions fixed in the `GenericPointGenerator` used in the elimination
